title: Prefer 'var' for local variable declarations
description: Using 'var' for local variable type inference improves code readability and reduces redundancy. It allows the compiler to infer the type, making code more concise and easier to maintain. Avoid explicit types when 'var' can be used without loss of clarity.
recommendation: Use 'var' for local variable declarations where the type is obvious and inference does not reduce clarity.

checks.message: Use 'var' instead of explicit types for local variable declarations when the type can be inferred from the context.
